one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG764-recommended wearable-based cardiac rehabilitation with direct RCT evidence of CV event risk reduction — using patented physical activity analytics, dedicated mentoring, and deployment across 13 NHS ICS regions.
why_it_matters_locally: KiActiv uses a proprietary wearable device to collect minute-by-minute physical activity data, combined with dedicated mentor support over a 12-week programme. With deployment across 13 NHS ICS regions and an RCT presented at the European Society of Cardiology Congress, it offers a data-rich approach to CR with broad NHS adoption.
target_problem_statement: Low CR uptake, limited objective physical activity measurement in CR programmes, and lack of personalised activity-based rehabilitation outside centre-based settings.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Conditionally recommended — one of four platforms with direct CV event risk reduction evidence

evidence_summary: RCT conducted with Liverpool University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust, presented at ESC Congress Amsterdam and published in the European Heart Journal. Liverpool NHS CR pilot (17 patients) showed 1.10-2.92 MET CRF improvements, equating to 14-38% reductions in all-cause mortality. HF virtual ward pilot: 78% of patients improved everyday physical activity. NICE identifies KiActiv as one of four platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: KiActiv RCT — Liverpool University Hospitals NHS FT
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: RCT results presented at the European Society of Cardiology Congress in Amsterdam and published in the European Heart Journal. NICE identifies KiActiv as one of four platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Full publication details (authors, sample size, DOI) not available in the dossier. Verify with KiActiv for the specific citation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: RCT reported as published in European Heart Journal and presented at ESC Congress. Exact citation details not provided in dossier — verify with Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS CR Pilot (2020–2021)
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust, 2020–2021
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 17 patients. Estimated 1.10–2.92 MET CRF improvements, equating to 14–38% reductions in all-cause mortality. Improved physical activity awareness and confidence.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Small pilot study (n=17). MET improvements are estimates based on activity data, not directly measured mortality outcomes. Service evaluation, not a peer-reviewed RCT.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Mortality reduction figures (14–38%) are modelled estimates from MET improvements, not directly observed outcomes. Small sample size (n=17).

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Heart failure virtual ward pilot — UHLG
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: UHLG NHS Trust — heart failure virtual ward
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 78% of patients improved everyday physical activity.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Service evaluation, not a peer-reviewed RCT. Sample size not stated in dossier.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Conditionally recommended. KiActiv assessed in two studies including one RCT. NICE identifies it as one of four platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk. EAG economic model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Recommendation conditional on 3-year evidence generation period.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: NICE EAG economic model — cardiac rehabilitation platforms
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: EAG model found digital cardiac rehabilitation platforms (including KiActiv) cost-saving compared with conventional rehabilitation. KiActiv reports 46,456 additional hours of physical activity above baseline across all participants, with improved QoL, mental wellbeing, and reduced GP/hospital visits.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Generic model across all HTG764 platforms. Aggregate activity hours figure is supplier-reported, not independently verified.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: ESC Congress Amsterdam — KiActiv RCT presentation
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: European Society of Cardiology Congress, Amsterdam
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: RCT results presented at ESC Congress. Also presented at BACPR Annual Conference. Evidence presented across multiple international cardiology forums.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Conference presentation — abstract/poster, not a full peer-reviewed publication. Verify published form with KiActiv.

clinical_evidence_detailed.type_label: Cross-pathway evidence (Long COVID, PR, chronic fatigue)
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Multiple NHS pathways — Long COVID, pulmonary rehabilitation, chronic fatigue, employee wellbeing
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Additional pilots conducted across Long COVID, pulmonary rehabilitation, chronic fatigue, and employee wellbeing pathways. R&D partnership with University of Bath on novel physical activity data analysis methods.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Cross-pathway evidence — not specific to cardiac rehabilitation. Individual study details not provided in dossier.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Evidence from non-cardiac pathways. Do not cite as direct cardiac rehabilitation evidence without cross-referencing specific study designs.

case_studies.title: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS CR pilot
case_studies.setting: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust, 2020-2021
case_studies.outcome: Estimated 1.10-2.92 MET CRF improvements, equating to 14-38% reductions in all-cause mortality.
case_studies.caveat: Small pilot study (n=17). MET improvements are estimates based on activity data.

case_studies.title: HF virtual ward pilot
case_studies.outcome: 78% of patients improved everyday physical activity.
case_studies.caveat: Service evaluation, not a peer-reviewed RCT. Health Innovation East impact story.

live_sites: Liverpool University Hospitals NHS FT, Suffolk and North East Essex ICS, NHS Gloucestershire ICS, Wiltshire Health and Care, Bath Centre for Fatigue Services. Deployed across 13 NHS ICS regions.
patients_covered_note: Deployed across 13 NHS ICS regions. Aggregate user numbers not publicly available.
dtac_note: Not within central DTAC records. DTAC approval is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation.
device_class: Uses a validated wearable activity monitor. Platform classification under NICE EVA.
cyber_notes: Confirm current security certifications and data handling for wearable device data.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd. Wearable device collects continuous physical activity data.
target_patients: Adults referred for cardiac rehabilitation — ACS, HF, cardiomyopathy, congenital heart disease, post-surgery. Also applicable to Long COVID, pulmonary rehabilitation, chronic fatigue.
exclusions: Referral and initial assessment by NHS CR team required. Patients must be able to wear the activity monitor.
onboarding_detail: Clinicians introduce KiActiv at initial assessment and confirm eligibility. KiActiv contacts the patient to complete enrolment and arrange start date. Wearable device provided to patients. 12-week programme with 6 KiActiv Mentor phone calls at key timepoints.
supplier_wrap: Wearable device, personalised online dashboard, 6 mentor phone calls over 12 weeks, educational resource signposting (nutrition, smoking cessation, psychosocial health).
local_wraparound_detail: Low local effort. Clinicians refer and confirm eligibility. KiActiv handles enrolment, device provision and mentor support.
training_note: Minimal — clinicians need to understand referral criteria and the KiActiv pathway.
monitoring_model: Patented technology evaluates continuous free-living activity data from wearable device. Personalised online dashboard available 24/7. KiActiv Mentor provides structured support calls.
monitoring_note: Remote activity monitoring via wearable device. Not a continuously monitored clinical service. Mentor calls at structured timepoints.
escalation_note: Mentor signposts to NHS services. Standard NHS escalation routes for clinical deterioration.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Mentor support during programme hours only.
implementation_prerequisites: Referral and initial assessment by NHS CR team
indicative_price_text: Service-based model; wearable device and mentor support included. Contact KiActiv for NHS pricing.
service_wrap_note: Wearable device, mentor support and online dashboard included in the service model.
contract_note: Contact Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: KiActiv reports 46,456 additional hours of physical activity above baseline across all participants, with improved QoL, mental wellbeing, and reduced GP/hospital visits. NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation.
tariff_considerations: Model using CR service costs and secondary prevention costs. Physical activity improvements have long-term CV risk reduction benefits.
roi_note: NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. Long-term ROI driven by CV risk reduction from sustained physical activity improvement.
minimum_conditions_for_success: NHS CR team referral pathway, patient able to wear wearable monitor, internet access.
procurement_notes: Contact Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd. Service-based model includes device and mentoring.

technical_integrations.device_integration: Proprietary wearable device collects minute-by-minute physical activity data. Patented analytics technology.
technical_integrations.data_hosting: Confirm with Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd

context_of_use.population: Adults with ACS, HF, cardiomyopathy, congenital heart disease, post-cardiac surgery
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Wearable-based physical activity monitoring with personalised feedback, mentor support, and educational signposting
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Minimal — clinician refers and confirms eligibility; KiActiv manages programme delivery
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG764 — conditionally recommended during 3-year evidence generation period

demo_notes: Contact Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd for a demonstration.
source_summary: NICE HTG764, ESC Congress presentation (European Heart Journal), Liverpool NHS CR pilot, Health Innovation East, kiactiv.com/our-evidence.
confidence_note: Content based on NICE guidance and publicly available information. DTAC approval must be obtained. Verify all details with Ki Performance Lifestyle Ltd before procurement.
